steps.description: Combine raw PDF- and web-scraped CSV records, aligning IDs and mapping EGFR variant formats into a single raw interim table.
Convert paper compound short-codes (e.g., 9a, 10b) from PDF extraction to valid chemical SMILES via the OPSIN API.
Remove invalid rows missing critical biological measurements (value, type, or units) or lacking both compound name and SMILES representation.
Convert all biological concentration units (uM, M, ug.mL-1, etc.) to nanomolar (nM), utilizing molecular weights calculated via RDKit for mass concentrations, and normalize mathematical relations.
Extract EGFR target mutations, assay types, cell lines, ATP concentrations, and covalent mechanism flags from free-text notes using regex parsing and RDKit SMARTS acrylamide substructure matches.
Standardize chemical structures: remove counter-ions and salts using RDKit's SaltRemover, compute canonical isomeric SMILES, and generate stable 2D InChIKeys.
Compute logarithmic pChEMBL scales (-log10(Molar value)) from standardized nM concentration values to provide uniform distribution metrics.
Group identical compound-variant-assay pairs. Unify data using median pChEMBL values if lab-to-lab variance is within 1.0 log-unit, and exclude highly conflicting entries.
Run programmatic verification checks via scripts/validate_project.py to validate the processed dataset against specs/validation_rules.json and dataset_schema.json.
